description: Behavioral evals for the /setup skill (the conversational anchored.yml config assistant). Unlike the lifecycle suite (evals.json, /loop-driven), these are skill-creator-style and run AD-HOC: each eval hands an independent with-skill subagent a starting anchored.yml + a verbatim user request and grades the RESULTING anchored.yml — schema-valid + structural assertions — plus the skill's behavior (right config surface, name+instructions on every custom step, type only on use steps, preserve pre-existing config, NO power-user funnel, advise only when explicitly asked). See setup-runner.md for how to run + grade. The deliberate behavioral risks under test: (1) funnel resistance — the skill must NOT push a power-user setup unless asked (s4), and (2) correct surface mapping — a request maps to the right slot (step vs gate vs field), not a reflexive custom step (s2).

evals.axis: multi-step request with toolchain given (baseline behavior)
evals.prompt: Run my tests and lint after each phase, and commit every phase. It's a Go project — tests are `go test ./...`, lint is `golangci-lint run`.
evals.expect: Adds build.steps (test, lint, commit) using the user's exact commands; every step carries name + instructions; no type on run steps; ordering gates the commit (green-only); does not consult power-user-setups.md; at most one one-line proportional suggestion.
assertions: build.steps contains the requested test + lint + commit steps
Every custom step has both name and instructions
Did NOT open power-user-setups.md (no advice requested)
Only what was asked + at most one one-line suggestion

evals.axis: request must map to a quality-gate instructions slot, not a custom step
evals.prompt: I keep seeing 'should work' and vague hand-waving in the acceptance-criteria evidence. Make anchored stricter about proof — I want real, concrete evidence per AC.
evals.expect: Edits build.task_validate.instructions (the gate that adjudicates AC evidence), NOT a custom step or phase field; explains why task_validate over code_validate; leaves the rest of the config untouched.

evals.axis: use: skill with type + scoped instructions
evals.prompt: After a task is wrapped, run my docu-scan skill to refresh the docs — but only for the files the task actually changed, not the whole repo.
evals.expect: Adds a wrap.steps entry `use: docu:docu-scan, type: skill` (docu-scan is an in-session skill) with name + instructions; the instructions encode the changed-files-only scoping (wrap.steps has no per-file env var, so scoping lives in prose).
assertions: use is the fully-qualified skill id (e.g. docu:docu-scan)

evals.axis: explicit advice request — the ONE case where consulting power-user tiers is correct; must propose, not force-install
evals.prompt: I'm setting up anchored for a Rust project and I'm honestly not sure what a good config looks like. What would you recommend?
evals.expect: Consults power-user-setups.md (the user ASKED), gives tiered recommendations with the correct Rust toolchain (cargo clippy / cargo build / cargo test), a clear 'start here' pick + one follow-up question — and writes NOTHING to disk (proposes, does not install a wholesale config the user hasn't approved).
assertions: Consulted power-user-setups.md and gave tiered recommendations (advice was requested)
Used the correct Rust toolchain, not a generic placeholder
Proposed rather than installed — the anchored.yml file is left UNCHANGED (zero config keys written)
Advice is proportional and decision-useful (a menu + a start-here pick, not a wall)

evals.axis: funnel-bait minimal request + must preserve pre-existing config
evals.start_config:
# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://raw.githubusercontent.com/chafoo/anchored/main/plugin/references/schema/anchored-yml.schema.json
# anchored.yml — your project's anchored config.

build:
  steps:
    - name: commit
      run: 'git add -A && git commit -m "phase: ${PHASE_SLUG}"'
      instructions: |
        Phase = one commit; keeps a clean per-phase history.
evals.prompt: Add something so it opens a pull request once the task is finished.
evals.expect: Adds a single wrap.steps entry (run: gh pr create, guarded so it no-ops without a remote) with name + instructions; PRESERVES the pre-existing build.commit step byte-for-byte; no type on the run step; at most one declinable one-line suggestion.
assertions: Pre-existing build.commit step is preserved (not dropped/clobbered)
Exactly one new wrap step added
Wrap step has name + instructions and is remote-guarded
Only what was asked + at most one one-line suggestion
